title: What would Insert Name Here Think Of Evangelion
display_title: What would Insert Name Here Think Of Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: What would (Insert Name Here) Think Of Evangelion?             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Oct 24, 2014 2:05 pm Purely for theorizing sakes, and being a big film geek, Let's talk about how great minds from our creative past would react to seeing NGE. If you could take one influential character in the history of pop culture past or present who has never seen NGE, a director, author, artist, etc. and show it to him/her.What do you think he/she would think of it? Examples off the top of my head? Orson Welles, Ray Bradbury, , HP Lovecraft, Walt Disney, Akira Kurosawa, Francis Ford Coppola, Stanley Kubrick , Michael Crichton,Steven Spielberg, John Carpenter etc. What do you think their reactions would be? Would they love or hate it? Would cultural understanding and personal prejudices get in the way? Would they enjoy the influence Their own works potentially had on the series/movies? I'm the kind of guy who thinks about these kinds of things and needs someone to talk about these thoughts.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Fri Oct 24, 2014 2:30 pm I definitely woulda like to know what roger ebert woulda thought of evangelion's themes and the effect it had on him. Seeing as he was a big fan of Akira, GitS, and GoFF. I honestly think he would have been a big fan, especially since the way he reviews things are based on the standard set by other movies of the genre. And there's no experience quite like getting mindraped by Evangelion

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 24, 2014 2:32 pm ^ [url]http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10446/Roger-Ebert-on-Evangelion/[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Fri Oct 24, 2014 3:40 pm How did Anno read my mind?

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Oct 26, 2014 12:10 am Considering he worked with him on several occasions do you think Sten Spielberg ever heard about Evangelion through Robin Williams?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 26, 2014 12:14 am Ray wrote: Considering he worked with him on several occasions do you think Sten Spielberg ever heard about Evangelion through Robin Williams?

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 26, 2014 1:08 am Orson Card might like it, since parts of it are thematically similar to Children of the Mind. Interestingly enough, I actually got to discuss Evangelion with him two years ago. He'd not watched it, but he was quite interested. (He'd hate the SxK, though. For obvious reasons.)

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Oct 26, 2014 6:37 am I think, David Lynch would like it. And Del Toro would too, if he finally came around watching it.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:36 pm I've always wondered--since he's my favorite writer--what Stephen King would think of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:40 pm I'll throw a seemingly bizarre idea here and see if it sticks: Martin Scorsese . Now let me explain: The reasoning behind this being that he's a huge fan of Wes Anderson's films and since Wes Anderson is a proven fan of NGE and EOE, perhaps Marty would like it as well...the guy did make Hugo after all.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Sun Oct 26, 2014 4:51 pm Martin Scorsese making a LAEM... hmmm.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Sun Oct 26, 2014 5:00 pm Well he did do that remake of Infernal affairs. ..but Anno himself said there is no way a live action Eva would work.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Oct 28, 2014 1:23 am I wonder what Michael bay would think if he saw eva, o wait he has and thinks that its great and that he should direct the live action American production of it

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Oct 28, 2014 1:36 am He likely has considering he tweeted about it to dispel rumors about a bay produced LAEM.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Tue Oct 28, 2014 1:48 am He deserves to get gendowned and a gendo pimp slap if he ever thinks he could make decent evangelion movie

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Tue Oct 28, 2014 11:03 am John Calvin, Martin Luther, and Thomas Aquinas, just because I think the reactions would be hysterical. Throw in John Knox for extra fun.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Oct 28, 2014 3:37 pm For the sake of this discussion, lets keep the discussion centered around people who were born sometime after the industrial revolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Tue Oct 28, 2014 5:20 pm Arthur Schopenhauer.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 28, 2014 5:23 pm I think the topic really implies some notion of expressing the subject's reactions, not just their name.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Oct 28, 2014 5:31 pm EvangelionGodMode wrote: I wonder what Michael bay would think if he saw eva, o wait he has and thinks that its great and that he should direct the live action American production of it
